essence: Insurance sells a promise: collect premiums now, pay claims later - profit comes from pricing risk right (underwriting) plus investing the 'float' in between.
Two profit engines: underwriting margin (premiums minus claims & costs) and investment income on the float.
The combined ratio is the scoreboard: below 100% you make money on underwriting; above it, you lean on investments.
It's a long-tail, capital-intensive, heavily regulated business where mispricing today surfaces as losses years later.
Distribution (agents, bancassurance, digital) and a claims-paying reputation decide who gathers premium cheaply.

definition.oneLiner: Companies that pool and price risk - collecting premiums to pay future claims, and investing the float in between.
definition.jobToBeDone: Protect people and businesses from financial shocks - death, illness, accident, property loss - by pooling risk and paying out when the insured event happens.

valueChain.profitPools: Profit pools sit in well-priced underwriting (especially protection, health and specialty) and in investment income on the float; distribution captures value via commissions, while commoditised motor/term lines run thin.
valueChain.powerShift: Value is shifting from agent-led distribution -> digital & embedded insurance -> data/IoT-driven personalised pricing, as insurtech and bancassurance reshape who owns the customer.

unitEconomics.unitOfRevenue: Premium per policy, plus investment income on the float
unitEconomics.revenueDrivers: Policies x Premium. Premium grows with new business, renewals/persistency, and pricing; investment income scales with the float and yields.

costStructure.shape: Cost is dominated by claims (an uncertain, delayed cost of goods) plus acquisition/commission and operating costs; the timing gap between premium and claim creates the investable float.
costStructure.leverage: Scale spreads fixed costs and improves risk pooling (law of large numbers), but the real swing factor is claims experience versus what was priced.

moats: Brand & trust (will they pay my claim?)
competitionAndMoats.concentration: Life is more concentrated (a dominant incumbent + strong private players); general and health are more fragmented, with standalone health insurers.

structuralForces.regulation: Heavy - the regulator (IRDAI in India) governs solvency, product approval, pricing, distribution and policyholder protection; solvency rules drive capital needs.
structuralForces.cyclicality: P&C has underwriting cycles (soft/hard markets as pricing and capacity swing); life is sensitive to interest rates, which affect guarantees and returns.
structuralForces.substitution: Self-insurance, government schemes, mutual models and capital-market alternatives (cat bonds) substitute at the margin; for savings-linked life, mutual funds compete for the rupee.
structuralForces.supplierBuyerPower: Reinsurers and distribution channels (banks, large agencies) hold bargaining power; corporate/group buyers negotiate hard; retail buyers have low individual power but high regulatory protection.

aiPenetration.maturity: Medium - strong momentum in claims, fraud and underwriting; pricing and distribution increasingly data-driven.
keyUseCases: Automated underwriting & risk pricing (incl. alternative data)
Telematics / IoT / wearables for usage-based pricing
aiPenetration.valueChainImpact: Biggest value in claims (the largest cost pool) and underwriting accuracy; IoT/telematics enables dynamic, personalised pricing that can reshape product economics.
opportunities: Expand cover to underserved risks via better data

ranges: Combined ratio: <100% = underwriting profit; ~95-105% common in P&C
Solvency ratio: >150% regulatory minimum (India); insurers run higher
Life new-business margin (VNB): ~20-30% for strong players
benchmarks.note: DIRECTIONAL - rules of thumb, not audited figures. Verify before quoting.

valueLevers: Improve underwriting / pricing accuracy (lower loss ratio)
Enhance investment returns on the float (within risk limits)
Cut claims leakage & fraud; automate operations

keyTerms.Premium: The price the insured pays for cover.
keyTerms.Combined ratio: Claims plus expenses divided by premiums; below 100% means an underwriting profit.
keyTerms.Loss ratio: Claims as a share of premiums.
keyTerms.Float: Premiums collected but not yet paid as claims - invested to earn income.
keyTerms.Underwriting: Selecting and pricing the risks the insurer agrees to cover.
keyTerms.Persistency: The share of policies that stay in force (renew) over time.
keyTerms.Reinsurance: Insurance bought by insurers to offload part of their risk.
keyTerms.Solvency ratio: Capital available versus required - the regulator's safety cushion.
keyTerms.VNB: Value of New Business - expected profit from policies sold in a period (life insurance).

commonCaseTypes: Product mix shift to higher-margin lines

questionsToAskFirst: Is the combined ratio above or below 100% - underwriting profit or loss?
Loss ratio vs expense ratio - where is the problem?
What's the distribution mix and its cost?
Product mix - protection vs savings vs commoditised lines?
rookieTraps: Chasing premium growth by underpricing risk (losses surface later)
Ignoring investment income vs the underwriting result
Treating life and P&C economics as the same

whatsUniqueHere: Insurance is the rare business that sells a promise and gets paid before it delivers - so its true cost of goods is unknown for years. That inversion makes actuarial pricing and reserving the heart of strategy, and turns the float (other people's money held before claims) into a powerful investment engine. Get the pricing wrong and the bill arrives long after the growth was celebrated.

indiaLandscape.marketStructure: Life insurance is led by the state-owned LIC alongside strong private players (HDFC Life, SBI Life, ICICI Prudential, Max Life); general insurance has public-sector insurers plus private (ICICI Lombard, Bajaj Allianz) and standalone health insurers (Star Health); GIC is the national reinsurer.
demandDrivers: Growing middle class & financialisation of savings
indiaLandscape.note: Semi-perishable - players & policy specifics refresh periodically.

similarTo: Asset management - investing pooled money for returns

currentState.note: PERISHABLE - refresh via research before quoting. Figures approximate.
currentState.marketStructure: India: LIC remains the life giant though private insurers keep gaining share; general & health insurance growing fast, led by health post-COVID; digital aggregators reshaping distribution; IRDAI pushing 'Insurance for All by 2047' and the Bima Sugam marketplace.
keyTrends: IRDAI reforms (Bima Sugam, higher FDI, composite-licence debate)
